tools.name: submit_query
tools.description:
Create a new CatchAll processing job from a natural-language query.

Use when:
- You want to start a new CatchAll web research run from a user query.
- You want the API to fetch/process sources and then return structured results.

Do not use when:
- You want status for an existing job (use `get_job_

tools.name: initialize_query
tools.description:
Preview suggested validators, enrichments, and date ranges before submitting.

Use when:
- You want to inspect/edit auto-generated validators/enrichments before submitting.
- You want to preview date adjustments via `date_modification_message`.

Do not use when:
- You want to start processing immedi

tools.name: get_job_status
tools.description:
Check the status of a submitted job.

Call this after submit_query to see if your job is ready.
Status progression: submitted -> analyzing -> fetching -> clustering -> enriching -> completed/failed

IMPORTANT: Jobs take several minutes to process.
First check after ~1-2 minutes, then poll every 30-6

tools.name: pull_results
tools.description:
Retrieve the results of a job.

Can be called before completion for partial results, or after completion
for the full set. Returns clustered, validated, and enriched web results.
While job status is active, call this repeatedly (typically page=1) to
refresh partial output. When job reaches completed

tools.name: pull_job_csv
tools.description:
Download a job's results as a CSV file.

Use when:
- You want the full job output as a CSV for offline analysis or export.
- Prefer this over `pull_results` when the consumer needs spreadsheet/CSV format.

tools.name: continue_job
tools.description:
Expand a job by processing more records beyond the initial limit.

This increases the number of records the system processes (which costs
additional credits). Only use this when the user wants MORE data processed.

This only applies to jobs originally submitted with `limit`.
If a job was submitted w

tools.name: list_user_jobs
tools.description:
List all jobs submitted by you.

Returns your job history with IDs, queries, statuses, and timestamps.

tools.name: delete_job
tools.description:
Permanently delete a job and its results.

Use when:
- You want to remove a job you no longer need from your account.

tools.name: validate_query
tools.description:
Check the quality of a query before submitting a job ("Check Query Quality").

Use when:
- You want quick feedback on whether a query is well-formed for CatchAll
  before spending credits on a job.
- You want concrete suggestions to improve a vague or overly broad query.

Do not use when:
- You want

tools.name: create_monitor
tools.description:
Create a recurring monitor from a completed job.

Monitors re-run a job's query on a schedule. Use the explore -> refine -> automate
pattern: submit a job, refine until results match, then create a monitor.

The schedule is defined in natural language (e.g., 'every day at 9 AM EST').
Always include

tools.name: list_monitors
tools.description:
List all your monitors.

Returns all monitors with their schedule, status, reference query, and webhook config.

tools.name: pull_monitor_results
tools.description:
Retrieve the latest results from a monitor.

Returns the most recent run's results including run_info, records, and all_records.

tools.name: pull_monitor_csv
tools.description:
Download the latest monitor run's results as a CSV file.

Use when:
- You want the most recent monitor run output as a CSV for offline analysis or export.
- Prefer this over `pull_monitor_results` when the consumer needs spreadsheet/CSV format.

tools.name: list_monitor_jobs
tools.description:
List all jobs spawned by a monitor.

Returns the history of scheduled runs for a monitor.

tools.name: disable_monitor
tools.description:
Disable a monitor to stop its scheduled runs.

The monitor can be re-enabled later with enable_monitor.

tools.name: enable_monitor
tools.description: Enable a previously disabled monitor to resume its scheduled runs.

tools.name: update_monitor
tools.description:
Update a monitor's webhook assignments and per-run limit.

Note: schedule and reference_job_id cannot be modified through this endpoint.
Webhooks are centralized — pass webhook IDs (from `create_webhook`/`list_webhooks`).

tools.name: delete_monitor
tools.description:
Permanently delete a monitor and stop its scheduled runs.

Use when:
- You want to remove a monitor entirely (use `disable_monitor` to only pause it).

tools.name: get_monitor_status
tools.description:
Get the status history of a monitor.

Use when:
- You want to see the timeline of a monitor's state changes (e.g. active,
  disabled, errored) and any related details.

tools.name: list_webhooks
tools.description:
List all your webhooks.

Use when:
- You want to see all webhook endpoints configured in your account.
- You need to find a webhook_id to pass to monitors (via webhook_ids) or jobs.

tools.name: create_webhook
tools.description:
Create a new webhook endpoint.

Use when:
- You want to register a URL to receive job or monitor result deliveries.
- You need a webhook_id to attach to a monitor (via webhook_ids) or a job submission.

tools.name: get_webhook
tools.description:
Retrieve the full configuration of a specific webhook.

Use when:
- You want to inspect a webhook's URL, method, headers, or status by its ID.

tools.name: update_webhook
tools.description:
Update an existing webhook's configuration.

Use when:
- You want to change a webhook's URL, method, headers, or other settings.
- You want to enable or disable a webhook (set `is_active`).
- Only the fields you provide are updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

tools.name: delete_webhook
tools.description:
Permanently delete a webhook endpoint.

Use when:
- You want to remove a webhook from your account.

tools.name: test_webhook
tools.description:
Send a test delivery to a webhook endpoint.

Use when:
- You want to verify a webhook URL is reachable and correctly configured
  before attaching it to a monitor or job.

tools.name: assign_webhook_resource
tools.description:
Map a resource (job, monitor, or monitor_group) to a webhook.

Use when:
- You want a webhook to fire for a specific job or monitor's deliveries.

tools.name: list_webhook_resources
tools.description:
List the resources mapped to a webhook.

Use when:
- You want to see which jobs/monitors a webhook is attached to.

tools.name: remove_webhook_resource
tools.description:
Unmap a resource from a webhook.

Use when:
- You want to stop a webhook from firing for a specific job or monitor.

tools.name: list_resource_webhooks
tools.description:
List the webhooks mapped to a specific resource (job/monitor/monitor_group).

Use when:
- You have a job or monitor ID and want to know which webhooks will fire for it.

tools.name: get_webhook_history
tools.description:
Get the webhook delivery history for a resource (job/monitor/monitor_group).

Use when:
- You want to see past webhook delivery attempts and their outcomes for a
  specific job or monitor.

tools.name: trigger_webhook
tools.description:
Manually trigger webhook delivery for a resource (job/monitor/monitor_group).

Use when:
- You want to (re-)send a webhook delivery on demand instead of waiting for
  the automatic dispatch — e.g. to replay a missed or failed delivery.

tools.name: create_dataset
tools.description:
Create a new dataset.

Datasets are collections of entities (companies/people). Connect a dataset to
a job via `submit_query(connected_dataset_ids=[...])` to narrow retrieval scope.

tools.name: list_datasets
tools.description: List your datasets.

tools.name: get_dataset
tools.description: Get a single dataset's details.

tools.name: update_dataset
tools.description: Update a dataset's name and/or description.

tools.name: delete_dataset
tools.description:
Permanently delete a dataset.

The entities the dataset referenced are not deleted; only the dataset and its
entity associations are removed.

tools.name: add_dataset_entities
tools.description: Add existing entities to a dataset.

tools.name: remove_dataset_entities
tools.description: Remove entities from a dataset (the entities themselves are not deleted).

tools.name: list_dataset_entities
tools.description: List the entities contained in a dataset.

tools.name: get_dataset_status
tools.description: Get the status history of a dataset (e.g. its enrichment progress over time).

tools.name: create_dataset_from_csv
tools.description:
Create a new dataset by uploading a CSV file.

The CSV must have at least a `name` column. For meaningful entity
enrichment each row should also include a `domain` column or a
`description` column (or both) — a row with only a name is accepted but
produces lower-quality enrichment. Additional column

tools.name: append_csv_to_dataset
tools.description:
Append entities from a CSV file to an existing dataset.

Parses the CSV and appends its entities to the dataset. Each row must
have a `name` column; include a `domain` or `description` column (or both)
for meaningful enrichment. Duplicate rows (by name) are skipped. To create
a new dataset from a CS

tools.name: create_entity
tools.description:
Create a single entity (a company or person).

``name`` is required plus at least one identifying
field: either ``description`` or ``additional_attributes.company_attributes.domain``.

tools.name: list_entities
tools.description: List your entities.

tools.name: create_entities_batch
tools.description: Create multiple entities in one call.

tools.name: get_entity
tools.description: Get a single entity's details.

tools.name: update_entity
tools.description: Update an entity's name, description, external_entity_id, and/or attributes.

tools.name: delete_entity
tools.description: Permanently delete an entity.

tools.name: create_project
tools.description:
Create a new project.

Projects group related resources (jobs, monitors, datasets, monitor_groups)
so you can organize work and filter listings by `project_id`.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: List your projects.

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Get a single project's details.

tools.name: update_project
tools.description:
Update a project's name and/or description.

Only the fields you provide are changed.

tools.name: delete_project
tools.description:
Delete a project.

By default the project's resources (jobs, monitors, etc.) are detached but
kept. Set `delete_resources=true` to also delete the contained resources.

tools.name: get_project_overview
tools.description: Get a project's resource overview (counts grouped by resource type and status).

tools.name: add_project_resources
tools.description: Add one or more resources to a project.

tools.name: list_project_resources
tools.description: List the resources contained in a project.

tools.name: remove_project_resource
tools.description: Remove a single resource from a project.

tools.name: get_user_limits
tools.description:
Retrieve plan features and current usage limits for your API key.

Use when:
- You want to know how many records/jobs/monitors your plan allows.
- You want to check current usage against plan limits before running a large job.

tools.name: check_health
tools.description:
Check API health status.

This tool maps to GET /health and does not require an API key.

tools.name: get_version
tools.description:
Get current API version.

This tool maps to GET /version and does not require an API key.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "submit_query" is 2775 chars, far longer than this server's median (123). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Create a new CatchAll processing job from a natural-language query. Use when: - You want to start a new CatchAll web research run from a user query. - You want …

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "submit_query" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

evidence.snippet: query, api_key, context, limit, start_date, end_date, validators, enrichments, mode, project_id, webhook_ids, schema, connected_dataset_ids, ed_score_min, ed_association_type, fetch_all_watchlist_news

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "get_job_status" is 1086 chars, far longer than this server's median (123). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Check the status of a submitted job. Call this after submit_query to see if your job is ready. Status progression: submitted -> analyzing -> fetching -> cluster…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "create_monitor" is 731 chars, far longer than this server's median (123). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Create a recurring monitor from a completed job. Monitors re-run a job's query on a schedule. Use the explore -> refine -> automate pattern: submit a job, refin…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: submit_query, create_webhook, get_webhook · Private data: submit_query, pull_job_csv, continue_job, pull_monitor_results, pull_monitor_csv, create_dataset_from_csv, append_csv_to_dataset, get_user_limits, check_health, get_version · Public sink: submit_query, create_monitor, list_monitors, update_monitor, list_webhooks, create_webhook, get_webhook, update_webhook, delete_webhook, test_webhook, assign_webhook_resource, list_webhook_resources, remove_webhook_resource, get_webhook_history, trigger_webhook · Destructive: submit_query, pull_job_csv, delete_job, validate_query, create_monitor, pull_monitor_results, pull_monitor_csv, update_monitor, delete_monitor, delete_webhook, remove_webhook_resource, delete_dataset, remove_dataset_entities, delete_entity, delete_project, remove_project_resource.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: submit_query, create_webhook, get_webhook · Private data: submit_query, pull_job_csv, continue_job, pull_monitor_results, pull_monitor_csv, create_dataset_from_csv, append_csv_to_dataset, get_user_limits, check_health, get_version · Public sink: submit_query, create_monitor, list_monitors, update_monitor, list_webhooks, create_webhook, get_webhook, update_webhook, delete_webhook, test_webhook, assign_webhook_resource, list_webhook_resources, remove_webhook_resource, get_webhook_history, trigger_webhook · Destructive: submit_query, pull_job_csv, delete_job, validate_query, create_monitor, pull_monitor_results, pull_monitor_csv, update_monitor, delete_monitor, delete_webhook, remove_webhook_resource, delete_dataset, remove_dataset_entities, delete_entity, delete_project, remove_project_resource

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
